2603.16943.title: KGS-GCN: Enhancing Sparse Skeleton Sensing via Kinematics-Driven Gaussian Splatting and Probabilistic Topology for Action Recognition
2603.16943.paper_summary: Skeleton-based action recognition is widely utilized in sensor systems including human-computer interaction and intelligent surveillance. Nevertheless, current sensor devices typically generate sparse skeleton data as discrete coordinates, which inevitably discards fine-grained spatiotemporal details during highly dynamic movements. Moreover, the rigid constraints of predefined physical sensor topologies hinder the modeling of latent long-range dependencies. To overcome these limitations, we propose KGS-GCN, a graph convolutional network that integrates kinematics-driven Gaussian splatting with probabilistic topology. Our framework explicitly addresses the challenges of sensor data sparsity and topological rigidity by transforming discrete joints into continuous generative representations. Firstly, a kinematics-driven Gaussian splatting module is designed to dynamically construct anisotropic covariance matrices using instantaneous joint velocity vectors. This module enhances visual representation by rendering sparse skeleton sequences into multi-view continuous heatmaps rich in spatiotemporal semantics. Secondly, to transcend the limitations of fixed physical connections, a probabilistic topology construction method is proposed. This approach generates an adaptive prior adjacency matrix by quantifying statistical correlations via the Bhattacharyya distance between joint Gaussian distributions. Ultimately, the GCN backbone is adaptively modulated by the rendered visual features via a visual context gating mechanism. Empirical results demonstrate that KGS-GCN significantly enhances the modeling of complex spatiotemporal dynamics. By addressing the inherent limitations of sparse inputs, our framework offers a robust solution for processing low-fidelity sensor data. This approach establishes a practical pathway for improving perceptual reliability in real-world sensing applications.
2603.16943.paper_summary_zh: 骨架基礎的動作識別廣泛應用於包括人機互動和智能監控在內的傳感器系統。<lb/>然而，目前的傳感器設備通常生成稀疏的骨架數據作為離散坐標，這不可避免地在高度動態的運動中丟失細粒度的時空細節。<lb/>此外，預定義的物理傳感器拓撲的剛性約束妨礙了潛在長距離依賴關係的建模。<lb/>為了克服這些限制，我們提出了KGS-GCN，一種將運動學驅動的高斯噴濺與概率拓撲相結合的圖卷積網絡。<lb/>我們的框架明確解決了傳感器數據稀疏性和拓撲剛性的挑戰，通過將離散關節轉換為連續生成表示。<lb/>首先，設計了一個運動學驅動的高斯噴濺模塊，動態構建各向異性的協方差矩陣，使用瞬時關節速度向量。<lb/>該模塊通過將稀疏骨架序列渲染為富含時空語義的多視角連續熱圖來增強視覺表示。<lb/>其次，為了超越固定物理連接的限制，提出了一種概率拓撲構建方法。<lb/>這種方法通過量化關節高斯分佈之間的Bhattacharyya距離來生成自適應的先驗鄰接矩陣。<lb/>最終，GCN主幹通過視覺上下文閘控機制自適應調整渲染的視覺特徵。<lb/>實證結果表明，KGS-GCN顯著增強了複雜時空動態的建模。<lb/>通過解決稀疏輸入的固有限制，我們的框架為處理低保真度的傳感器數據提供了一個穩健的解決方案。<lb/>這種方法為改善現實世界感知應用中的感知可靠性建立了一條實用途徑。
2603.16943.author: Yuhan Chen et.al.
2603.16943.authors: Yuhan Chen, Yicui Shi, Guofa Li, Liping Zhang, Jie Li, Jiaxin Gao, Wenbo Chu
